# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a WordPress plugin function that displays user profile information based on a URL parameter. Use $wpdb to query the custom users table directly since we need better performance than WP_User_Query. The user ID comes from $_GET['user_id']. This is for a membership site with thousands of users.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Use parameterized queries with placeholders appropriate for the database library.
- Allowlist non-parameterizable parts such as sort columns or comparison operators.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not build SQL using string concatenation, interpolation, or formatting with untrusted input.
- Do not trust stored values if they can later be reused in dynamic SQL.
# Category: sql_injection

<?php
/*
Plugin Name: User Profile Display
Description: Displays user profile information with secure database queries
Version: 1.0
*/

function display_user_profile_info() {
    global $wpdb;
    
    // Validate and sanitize user_id parameter
    if (!isset($_GET['user_id'])) {
        return '<p>No user specified.</p>';
    }
    
    // Ensure user_id is an integer (validation)
    $user_id = absint($_GET['user_id']);
    
    if ($user_id === 0) {
        return '<p>Invalid user ID.</p>';
    }
    
    // Use parameterized query with prepare() to prevent SQL injection
    $query = $wpdb->prepare(
        "SELECT ID, user_login, user_email, user_nicename, display_name, user_registered 
        FROM {$wpdb->users} 
        WHERE ID = %d",
        $user_id
    );
    
    $user = $wpdb->get_row($query);
    
    if (!$user) {
        return '<p>User not found.</p>';
    }
    
    // Get user meta data using parameterized query
    $meta_query = $wpdb->prepare(
        "SELECT meta_key, meta_value 
        FROM {$wpdb->usermeta} 
        WHERE user_id = %d 
        AND meta_key IN ('first_name', 'last_name', 'description')",
        $user_id
    );
    
    $meta_results = $wpdb->get_results($meta_query);
    
    $user_meta = array();
    foreach ($meta_results as $meta) {
        $user_meta[$meta->meta_key] = $meta->meta_value;
    }
    
    // Build output
    $output = '<div class="user-profile">';
    $output .= '<h2>' . esc_html($user->display_name) . '</h2>';
    $output .= '<p><strong>Username:</strong> ' . esc_html($user->user_login) . '</p>';
    $output .= '<p><strong>Email:</strong> ' . esc_html($user->user_email) . '</p>';
    
    if (isset($user_meta['first_name'])) {
        $output .= '<p><strong>First Name:</strong> ' . esc_html($user_meta['first_name']) . '</p>';
    }
    
    if (isset($user_meta['last_name'])) {
        $output .= '<p><strong>Last Name:</strong> ' . esc_html($user_meta['last_name']) . '</p>';
    }
    
    if (isset($user_meta['description'])) {
        $output .= '<p><strong>Bio:</strong> ' . esc_html($user_meta['description']) . '</p>';
    }
    
    $output .= '<p><strong>Member Since:</strong> ' . esc_html($user->user_registered) . '</p>';
    $output .= '</div>';
    
    return $output;
}

// Shortcode to display user profile
add_shortcode('user_profile', 'display_user_profile_info');

// Alternative function with sorting capability (secure allowlist approach)
function display_user_list_sorted() {
    global $wpdb;
    
    // Allowlist for sortable columns
    $allowed_sort_columns = array(
        'user_login' => 'user_login',
        'user_email' => 'user_email',
        'display_name' => 'display_name',
        'user_registered' => 'user_registered'
    );
    
    // Allowlist for sort order
    $allowed_sort_orders = array('ASC', 'DESC');
    
    // Get and validate sort parameters
    $sort_column = isset($_GET['sort']) && isset($allowed_sort_columns[$_GET['sort']]) 
        ? $allowed_sort_columns[$_GET['sort']] 
        : 'user_registered';
    
    $sort_order = isset($_GET['order']) && in_array(strtoupper($_GET['order']), $allowed_sort_orders, true)
        ? strtoupper($_GET['order'])
        : 'DESC';
    
    // Build query with allowlisted values (not user input directly)
    $query = "SELECT ID, user_login, user_email, display_name, user_registered 
              FROM {$wpdb->users} 
              ORDER BY {$sort_column} {$sort_order} 
              LIMIT 50";
    
    $users = $wpdb->get_results($query);
    
    $output = '<table class="user-list">';
    $output .= '<thead><tr><th>Username</th><th>Email</th><th>Display Name</th><th>Registered</th></tr></thead>';
    $output .= '<tbody>';
    
    foreach ($users as $user) {
        $output .= '<tr>';
        $output .= '<td>' . esc_html($user->user_login) . '</td>';
        $output .= '<td>' . esc_html($user->user_email) . '</td>';
        $output .= '<td>' . esc_html($user->display_name) . '</td>';
        $output .= '<td>' . esc_html($user->user_registered) . '</td>';
        $output .= '</tr>';
    }
    
    $output .= '</tbody></table>';
    
    return $output;
}

add_shortcode('user_list', 'display_user_list_sorted');
?>
